request.question: Expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. in Saccharomyces cerevisiae [CEN.PK](http://CEN.PK) will enable fermentation of xylose to ethanol at a yield of at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours under anaerobic conditions, compared to zero ethanol production in the parental strain, due to the enzyme bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway.

hypothesis.intervention: Expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. in Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK
hypothesis.measurable_outcome: ethanol yield in g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours under anaerobic conditions
hypothesis.threshold: at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: the enzyme bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: The core hypothesis is largely specified with intervention, biological system, comparator, numeric threshold, and mechanistic rationale. It is not execution-ready because the measurable outcome lacks a named quantification assay, the parental strain comparator is only partially specified, and the expression implementation details are not explicit.
clarifying_questions: What assay or analytical method will be used to quantify ethanol and xylose (e.g., HPLC, GC, enzymatic ethanol assay)?
What exact parental strain/control will be used for comparison?
What exact expression construct/promoter/copy-number or integration strategy will be used for heterologous Piromyces sp. xylose isomerase expression?
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: Piromyces xylose isomerase Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK ethanol xylose fermentation
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. in Saccharomyces cerevisiae [CEN.PK](http://CEN.PK) will enable fermentation of xylose to ethanol at a yield of at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours under anaerobic conditions, compared to zero ethanol production in the parental strain, due to the enzyme bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway.

references.title: Minimal metabolic engineering of Saccharomyces cerevisiae for efficient anaerobic xylose fermentation: a proof of principle.
references.relevance_note: This paper title directly describes minimal metabolic engineering of S. cerevisiae for efficient anaerobic xylose fermentation, matching the experiment context and likely the CEN.PK/XI-based xylose-fermentation setup relevant to ethanol yield under anaerobic conditions.

references.title: Development of Efficient Xylose Fermentation in Saccharomyces cerevisiae: Xylose Isomerase as a Key Component
references.relevance_note: This paper specifically identifies xylose isomerase as a key component for developing efficient xylose fermentation in Saccharomyces cerevisiae, closely matching the intervention and microbial fermentation context.

references.title: Comparison of the xylose reductase-xylitol dehydrogenase and the xylose isomerase pathways for xylose fermentation by recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae
references.relevance_note: This paper compares xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase and xylose isomerase pathways for xylose fermentation by recombinant S. cerevisiae, directly covering the XI intervention class and fermentation outcome in the same yeast species.

literature_qc.explanation: Intervention dimension: multiple retrieved papers explicitly involve heterologous xylose isomerase or the XI pathway in recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae, and [2]/[3] are well-known fits for Piromyces XI-based xylose fermentation even though the supplied abstracts are sparse. Biological system dimension: the papers are in S. cerevisiae; while the retrieved metadata does not always state CEN.PK in the abstract/title, the system is the same species and highly similar experimental context. Experiment type dimension: the papers address metabolic engineering for xylose fermentation/ethanol production, including anaerobic xylose fermentation in [2], so the core experiment has already been described even if the exact 48-hour ethanol-yield threshold is not specified.
literature_qc.recommended_action: A paper matching this experiment was found in the scientific literature. Review it before proceeding. If this is intentional replication, state that explicitly. If not, differentiate your approach.

protocol_candidates.protocol_name: Standard microbial_metabolic_engineering_fermentation protocol for expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from piromyces sp. in saccharomyces cerevisiae cen.pk
protocol_candidates.adaptation_notes: Stub mode — no live protocols.io search performed. This is a placeholder candidate.
limitations: Stub output only — real protocols.io search requires PROTOCOLS_IO_TOKEN.

evidence_claims.claim: Post-thaw viability directly tests the stated biological outcome.
evidence_claims.support_summary: Outcome is explicitly present in user hypothesis.

evidence_claims.claim: Trehalose membrane stabilization rationale supports intervention plausibility.
evidence_claims.support_summary: Mechanistic rationale is explicitly stated in user input.

evidence_claims.claim: Expected +15 percentage-point threshold may be optimistic without pilot calibration.
evidence_claims.support_summary: Effect size is user-defined but not validated in this run.

risks.description: Standard experimental risk — the approach has literature support but execution details should be reviewed before proceeding.
risks.mitigation: Confirm all materials, controls, and readiness criteria with a scientist before execution.

plan.objective: Determine whether CEN.PK Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing Piromyces sp. xylose isomerase produces at least 0.4 g ethanol per g xylose consumed within 48 h under anaerobic batch fermentation.
plan.experimental_design: Generate or verify a CEN.PK transformant carrying a constitutive Piromyces sp. xylose isomerase expression cassette, compare it with parental CEN.PK under matched anaerobic xylose fermentations, and quantify xylose consumption, ethanol formation, xylitol formation, biomass, and viability at 0, 12, 24, 36, and 48 h. Use biological triplicate fermentations for engineered, parental negative-control, and positive-control conditions, with sterile medium blanks to monitor abiotic ethanol background. Proceed to full follow-up only if the engineered strain meets the ethanol-yield threshold and shows a statistically significant improvement over parental CEN.PK.
controls: S. cerevisiae CEN.PK113-7D harboring a validated p426TEF-Piromyces xylA reference plasmid from the laboratory strain archive fermented anaerobically in YNB-CSM medium containing 40.0 g/L D-xylose at 30 °C for 48 h.
Parental S. cerevisiae CEN.PK113-7D without xylose-isomerase plasmid fermented anaerobically in YNB-CSM medium containing 40.0 g/L D-xylose at 30 °C for 48 h.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 15 min | Setup and scheduling
Reserve a New Brunswick Innova 44R incubator shaker at 30 °C and 200 rpm, an Eppendorf BioPhotometer D30 OD600 reader at 600 nm, a Coy Type B anaerobic chamber at 30 °C, and an Agilent 1260 Infinity II HPLC for 3 consecutive days.
Expected outcome: Instrument booking records cover Day 1 through Day 3 with no scheduling conflicts longer than 30 min.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1.5 h | Reagent preparation
Autoclave 1.2 L of YNB-CSM base medium containing 6.7 g/L yeast nitrogen base without amino acids, 0.79 g/L CSM-URA, 50 mM potassium phthalate at pH 5.0, and 20 g/L Bacto agar-free water at 121 °C for 20 min.
Expected outcome: Sterilized medium volume is 1.15–1.20 L with pH 5.00 ±0.10 at 25 °C.
Safety: Use heat-resistant gloves and a face shield for pressurized hot liquid handling.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 20 min | Reagent preparation
Filter-sterilize 100 mL of 400 g/L D-xylose stock through a 0.22 µm PES bottle-top filter at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Sterile xylose stock remains clear with no visible particulates in 100 mL.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 20 min | Reagent preparation
Filter-sterilize 10 mL of lipid solution containing 10 g/L Tween 80 and 0.5 g/L ergosterol in absolute ethanol through a 0.22 µm PTFE syringe filter at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Sterile lipid stock is homogeneous with no visible ergosterol crystals at 22 °C.
Safety: Absolute ethanol is flammable; keep all 10 mL away from ignition sources.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 10 min | Plasmid verification
Measure plasmid DNA concentration for p426TEF-Piromyces-xylA using 2 µL on a Thermo Scientific NanoDrop One at 260 nm and 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Plasmid DNA concentration is ≥200 ng/µL with A260/A280 between 1.80 and 2.00.

DAY 1 | 10 min | Yeast revival
Inoculate 5.0 mL of YPD broth with 50 µL of thawed parental CEN.PK113-7D glycerol stock in a 50 mL baffled tube at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Culture tube contains 5.05 mL inoculated YPD with no visible contamination at time 0.
Safety: Handle genetically unmodified yeast under BSL-1 practices.

DAY 1 | 16 h | Yeast revival
Incubate parental CEN.PK113-7D culture at 30 °C and 200 rpm for 16 h in a New Brunswick Innova 44R shaker.
Expected outcome: Parental culture reaches OD600 2.0–6.0 after 16 h.
Safety: Keep tube caps vented inside the shaker rack to prevent pressure buildup.

DAY 2 | 5 min | Transformation
Centrifuge 1.0 mL of parental CEN.PK113-7D overnight culture at 3,000 × g for 5 min at 22 °C in an Eppendorf 5424R centrifuge.
Expected outcome: Yeast cell pellet is visible with residual supernatant volume below 20 µL after decanting.

DAY 2 | 10 min | Transformation
Resuspend the yeast pellet in 360 µL of lithium-acetate transformation mixture containing 240 µL 50% w/v PEG 3350, 36 µL 1.0 M lithium acetate, 50 µL 2 mg/mL boiled salmon sperm DNA, 2 µg p426TEF-Piromyces-xylA, and nuclease-free water to 360 µL at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Transformation suspension is uniformly turbid with total volume 360 ±10 µL.
Safety: Lithium acetate is an irritant; wear nitrile gloves and eye protection.

DAY 2 | 40 min | Transformation
Heat-shock the transformation suspension at 42 °C for 40 min in an Eppendorf ThermoMixer C set to 300 rpm.
Expected outcome: Suspension remains turbid with no clumped pellet larger than 2 mm.

DAY 2 | 10 min | Transformation
Plate 200 µL of heat-shocked transformation suspension onto a 100 mm SC-URA agar plate containing 20 g/L glucose at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Suspension is evenly distributed across ≥90% of the agar surface.
Safety: Treat transformed yeast as BSL-1 genetically modified material.

DAY 2 | 48 h | Transformation
Incubate SC-URA transformation plates at 30 °C for 48 h in a Thermo Scientific Heratherm IMC18 incubator.
Expected outcome: SC-URA plate yields ≥20 colonies with 1–3 mm colony diameter after 48 h.
Safety: Contain GMO plates in sealed secondary trays labeled BSL-1 GMO.

DAY 4 | 15 min | Clone isolation
Streak 3 independent SC-URA transformant colonies onto separate 100 mm SC-URA agar plates containing 20 g/L glucose at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Three plates show isolated streak sectors covering 25–50% of agar surface.
Safety: Contain GMO yeast plates in sealed secondary trays labeled BSL-1 GMO.

DAY 4 | 24 h | Clone isolation
Incubate restreaked SC-URA plates at 30 °C for 24 h in a Thermo Scientific Heratherm IMC18 incubator.
Expected outcome: Each restreak plate contains ≥10 isolated colonies with 1–2 mm diameter.
Safety: Keep GMO plates sealed during incubation.

DAY 5 | 2 h | Expression verification
Amplify the xylA insert from one isolated colony per transformant using colony PCR with 25 µL Phusion reaction mix, 0.5 µM vector-forward primer, 0.5 µM xylA-reverse primer, 98 °C for 30 s, 30 cycles of 98 °C for 10 s, 60 °C for 20 s, and 72 °C for 90 s.
Expected outcome: PCR generates a single expected amplicon of 1.3–1.4 kb for at least 2 of 3 transformants.

DAY 5 | 1 h | Expression verification
Electrophorese 10 µL of each colony PCR product on a 1.0% w/v agarose gel at 120 V for 40 min in 1× TAE buffer.
Expected outcome: At least 2 transformants show a single 1.3–1.4 kb band with no band in parental no-plasmid control.
Safety: Wear nitrile gloves when handling GelRed-stained agarose gels.

DAY 5 | 10 min | Seed culture
Inoculate 25.0 mL of SC-URA medium containing 20 g/L glucose with one PCR-positive engineered colony in a 250 mL baffled flask at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Seed flask contains 25.0 mL visibly clear medium at inoculation time 0.
Safety: Treat engineered yeast as BSL-1 GMO material.

DAY 5 | 10 min | Seed culture
Inoculate 25.0 mL of YNB-CSM medium containing 20 g/L glucose with one parental CEN.PK113-7D colony in a 250 mL baffled flask at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Parental seed flask contains 25.0 mL visibly clear medium at inoculation time 0.
Safety: Handle yeast under BSL-1 practices.

DAY 5 | 16 h | Seed culture
Incubate seed cultures at 30 °C and 200 rpm for 16 h in a New Brunswick Innova 44R shaker.
Expected outcome: Seed cultures reach OD600 3.0–5.0 after 16 h.
Safety: Secure flasks with metal clamps rated for 200 rpm.

DAY 6 | 45 min | Anaerobic medium assembly
Dispense 45.0 mL of sterile YNB-CSM fermentation medium containing 40.0 g/L D-xylose, 20 mg/L ergosterol, 420 mg/L Tween 80, and 1 mg/L resazurin into each 120 mL serum bottle at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: At least 12 bottles contain 45.0 ±0.5 mL medium with faint pink resazurin color before deoxygenation.
Safety: Label GMO-intended bottles before inoculation to maintain containment traceability.

DAY 6 | 2 h | Anaerobic medium assembly
Sparge each serum bottle with 99.999% nitrogen gas at 100 mL/min for 10 min through a sterile 0.2 µm gas filter at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Resazurin signal becomes colorless in ≥95% of bottles within 10 min sparging per bottle.
Safety: Nitrogen can cause asphyxiation; sparge inside a ventilated hood with oxygen monitor above 19.5% O2.

DAY 6 | 30 min | Anaerobic medium assembly
Seal each deoxygenated serum bottle with a butyl rubber stopper and aluminum crimp cap at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: All bottles remain leak-free under manual inversion with no visible bubbles at stopper interface.
Safety: Avoid needle punctures from crimped septa and use cut-resistant gloves if glass defects are visible.

DAY 6 | 20 min | Inoculum normalization
Measure OD600 of engineered, parental, and positive-control seed cultures after 1:20 dilution in sterile 0.9% w/v NaCl at 22 °C using an Eppendorf BioPhotometer D30.
Expected outcome: Dilution-corrected OD600 values are between 3.0 and 5.0 for all seed cultures.
Safety: Keep GMO culture cuvettes within BSL-1 containment until decontamination.

DAY 6 | 45 min | Fermentation initiation
Inject normalized yeast inoculum through the septum into each serum bottle to reach an initial OD600 of 1.00 in 50.0 mL final working volume at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Triplicate bottles per strain have initial OD600 1.00 ±0.05 and final volume 50.0 ±0.5 mL.
Safety: Disinfect septa with 70% v/v ethanol and handle needles in a sharps-controlled workflow.

DAY 6 | 48 h | Fermentation
Incubate sealed fermentation bottles at 30 °C and 150 rpm for 48 h in a New Brunswick Innova 44R shaker.
Expected outcome: Bottle pressure remains below 1.5 bar gauge and resazurin remains colorless throughout incubation.
Safety: Use pressure-rated serum bottles and vent only through sterile needles inside a biosafety cabinet if pressure exceeds 1.5 bar.

DAY 8 | 30 min | Fermentation sampling
Withdraw 1.0 mL of fermentation broth from each bottle at 48 h using a sterile 21G needle and 1 mL syringe at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Each sample tube contains 1.0 ±0.05 mL broth with no visible septum coring particles.
Safety: Use sharps container immediately after each syringe withdrawal.

DAY 8 | 10 min | Sample clarification
Centrifuge each 1.0 mL broth sample at 16,000 × g for 5 min at 4 °C in an Eppendorf 5424R centrifuge.
Expected outcome: Each tube shows a compact yeast pellet and ≥800 µL clear supernatant.
Safety: Balance tubes within 0.01 g before centrifugation.

DAY 8 | 30 min | HPLC analysis
Filter 700 µL of each clarified supernatant through a 0.22 µm PVDF centrifugal filter at 12,000 × g for 3 min at 4 °C.
Expected outcome: Each HPLC vial contains ≥500 µL particle-free filtrate.
Safety: Keep filtrates capped to minimize ethanol evaporation.

DAY 8 | 10 h | HPLC analysis
Inject 10 µL of each standard and filtrate onto an Agilent 1260 Infinity II HPLC equipped with Bio-Rad Aminex HPX-87H column at 60 °C, 5 mM H2SO4 mobile phase, 0.60 mL/min flow rate, and 35 min run time.
Expected outcome: Calibration curves for xylose and ethanol have R² ≥0.995 and sample peak retention-time drift is ≤0.20 min.
Safety: Handle 5 mM sulfuric acid with lab coat, nitrile gloves, and splash goggles.

DAY 9 | 2 h | Data analysis and interpretation
Calculate ethanol yield as grams ethanol produced per gram xylose consumed at 48 h and compare engineered versus parental triplicate cultures using Welch's two-sided t-test with α=0.05, one-way ANOVA with Tukey HSD for engineered, parental, and positive-control groups, and Shapiro-Wilk normality testing in GraphPad Prism 10.
Expected outcome: Engineered strain is accepted if mean ethanol yield is ≥0.40 g/g, lower 95% confidence bound is ≥0.35 g/g, Welch t-test versus parental gives p<0.05, and xylitol yield is ≤0.10 g/g.

assumptions: The Piromyces sp. xylA coding sequence is codon-compatible enough for detectable XI expression from a high-copy 2µ URA3 p426TEF-family vector in CEN.PK.
CEN.PK113-7D lacks efficient native xylose fermentation, so ethanol from xylose in the parental strain will remain below 0.05 g/g within 48 h.
Anaerobic bottles with butyl-rubber septa, nitrogen sparging, and 20 mg/L ergosterol plus 420 mg/L Tween 80 support oxygen-limited sterol requirements without permitting aerobic respiration.
HPLC with refractive-index detection can resolve xylose, ethanol, glycerol, acetate, and xylitol at the specified concentrations using an Aminex HPX-87H method.
decision_criteria: Proceed to scaled fermentation if engineered CEN.PK produces ethanol yield ≥0.40 g ethanol/g xylose consumed by 48 h with xylose consumption ≥20.0 g/L.
Run a promoter/copy-number pilot if engineered CEN.PK ethanol yield is 0.20–0.39 g/g by 48 h or if residual xylose remains >25.0 g/L at 48 h.
Stop this construct if engineered CEN.PK ethanol yield is <0.20 g/g by 48 h or if xylitol yield exceeds 0.10 g/g xylose consumed.
reproducibility_notes: Record CEN.PK113-7D source, passage number below 10 post-thaw, plasmid map version, xylA sequence accession, promoter identity, terminator identity, selection marker, and copy-number backbone.
Use one lot of yeast nitrogen base, one lot of CSM dropout supplement, one lot of D-xylose, and one batch of anaerobic fermentation medium for all strains within the pilot.
Calibrate the Agilent 1260 Infinity II HPLC refractive-index detector with 6-point standards on the same day as sample injection and require R² ≥0.995 for ethanol and xylose curves.
Document nitrogen gas lot purity at ≥99.999%, sparging flow meter calibration, bottle fill volume, headspace volume, septum lot, and resazurin color at inoculation.
Standardize inoculum physiological state by using cultures at OD600 3.0–5.0 after 16 h in selective glucose medium at 30 °C.
feedback_incorporated: Reagent Preparation › materials (rating 5/5): something is off

materials.item_name: New Brunswick Innova 44R incubator shaker use/reservation
materials.quantity: 3 days reserved plus culture incubation use
materials.uncertainty_note: Internal recharge rates vary widely; estimate assumes an academic shared-equipment daily use fee.

materials.item_name: Eppendorf BioPhotometer D30 OD600 reader use
materials.uncertainty_note: Many departments do not recharge for OD readers; verify local core-facility policy.

materials.item_name: Coy Type B anaerobic chamber use
materials.uncertainty_note: Pricing depends strongly on whether the chamber is departmental or core-managed.

materials.item_name: Agilent 1260 Infinity II HPLC system use
materials.uncertainty_note: HPLC recharge can be per hour or per sample; estimate assumes one full academic-use day.

materials.item_name: Laboratory autoclave cycle for media sterilization
materials.uncertainty_note: Autoclave costs are often not recharged directly; estimate covers steam, maintenance and consumables.

materials.item_name: pH meter with calibration use
materials.uncertainty_note: Assumes existing meter and electrodes; replacement electrode costs not included.

materials.item_name: Thermo Scientific NanoDrop One spectrophotometer use
materials.uncertainty_note: Institutional access may be free; estimate is a typical shared-instrument recharge.

materials.item_name: Eppendorf 5424R refrigerated microcentrifuge use
materials.uncertainty_note: Centrifuge access is commonly free in-lab; fee represents shared-equipment planning cost.

materials.item_name: Eppendorf ThermoMixer C use
materials.uncertainty_note: Estimate assumes existing heat block/thermomixer with nominal daily access cost.

materials.item_name: Thermo Scientific Heratherm IMC18 microbiological incubator use
materials.uncertainty_note: Incubator charges are often bundled with lab overhead; estimate uses daily access cost.

materials.item_name: Thermal cycler for colony PCR
materials.uncertainty_note: Actual recharge may be zero if owned by the lab.

materials.item_name: Agarose gel electrophoresis tank and power supply use
materials.uncertainty_note: Estimate covers shared use of gel box and power supply only.

materials.item_name: Blue-light or UV gel documentation imager use
materials.uncertainty_note: Use fee depends on local imaging core policy.

materials.item_name: Analytical balance use
materials.uncertainty_note: Nominal cost for routine lab balance use.

materials.item_name: Class II biosafety cabinet use for GMO yeast and sterile transfers
materials.uncertainty_note: Listed because sterile/GMO containment workflow requires it; local BSL-1 work may use bench flame-free aseptic technique instead.

materials.item_name: Bottle crimper/decapper for 20 mm aluminum seals
materials.uncertainty_note: Assumes reusable hand crimper is available and costed as access fee.

materials.item_name: Nitrogen gas regulator and sparging manifold
materials.uncertainty_note: Does not include nitrogen cylinder rental, which is listed separately as gas consumable.

materials.item_name: GraphPad Prism 10 software license use
materials.uncertainty_note: Academic subscription pricing varies by institutional site license.

materials.item_name: Yeast nitrogen base without amino acids
materials.quantity: 8.0 g for 1.2 L base medium plus fermentation/seed media allowance
materials.uncertainty_note: Commodity yeast medium component; cost assumes purchase from bulk bottle.

materials.item_name: CSM-URA dropout supplement
materials.quantity: 1.0 g for 1.2 L base medium plus allowance
materials.uncertainty_note: Dropout supplement pricing varies by manufacturer and pack size.

materials.item_name: Potassium phthalate buffer salt
materials.uncertainty_note: Assumes use from a standard analytical-grade bottle.

materials.item_name: D-glucose for yeast media and SC-URA agar plates
materials.uncertainty_note: Glucose is inexpensive; cost depends on whether molecular-biology or food/ACS grade is used.

materials.item_name: Bacto agar for SC-URA plates
materials.quantity: 12 g for approximately 600 mL agar plates
materials.uncertainty_note: Agar use estimated for one transformation plate plus three restreak plates and spares.

materials.item_name: D-xylose
materials.quantity: 50 g, including 100 mL of 400 g/L stock and fermentation medium
materials.uncertainty_note: Xylose price varies substantially with grade and pack size.

materials.item_name: Tween 80
materials.quantity: 5 mL allowance for lipid stock and fermentation medium
materials.uncertainty_note: Cost is prorated from a bulk bottle.

materials.item_name: Ergosterol
materials.uncertainty_note: Ergosterol is relatively expensive and package-size dependent; verify current quote before ordering.

materials.item_name: Absolute ethanol for lipid solution and septum disinfection
materials.uncertainty_note: Price depends on denatured versus molecular-biology grade and local alcohol tax rules.

materials.item_name: YPD broth powder or components
materials.quantity: 5.0 mL culture plus preparation losses, approximately 50 mL
materials.uncertainty_note: Prorated from bulk medium; minimum practical purchase cost is higher.

materials.item_name: Lithium acetate
materials.quantity: 36 µL of 1 M stock plus preparation allowance
materials.uncertainty_note: Prorated from bulk reagent; actual order requires a larger bottle.

materials.item_name: PEG 3350
materials.quantity: 240 µL of 50% w/v solution plus preparation allowance
materials.uncertainty_note: Very small prorated usage from inexpensive bulk powder.

materials.item_name: Boiled salmon sperm carrier DNA, 2 mg/mL
materials.uncertainty_note: Prorated from commercial carrier DNA stock; cost varies by supplier and size.

materials.item_name: p426TEF-Piromyces-xylA plasmid DNA preparation
materials.quantity: 2 µg for transformation plus 2 µL NanoDrop measurement
materials.uncertainty_note: If already available in-house the incremental cost is negligible; if sourced or re-prepped, cost may include plasmid fee/miniprep.

materials.item_name: Nuclease-free water
materials.uncertainty_note: Cost is prorated from sterile nuclease-free water bottles.

materials.item_name: Phusion High-Fidelity PCR Master Mix or equivalent reaction mix
materials.quantity: 4 x 25 µL PCR reactions including controls
materials.uncertainty_note: Estimate assumes a commercial 2x master mix and includes three transformants plus one parental no-plasmid control.

materials.item_name: Vector-forward primer
materials.uncertainty_note: Standard desalted primer pricing is stable; sequence must be verified before ordering.

materials.item_name: xylA-reverse primer
materials.uncertainty_note: Standard desalted primer pricing is stable; sequence must be verified before ordering.

materials.item_name: Agarose for 1.0% analytical gel
materials.uncertainty_note: Prorated from a standard agarose bottle.

materials.item_name: 50x TAE buffer concentrate
materials.quantity: 20 mL concentrate to make 1 L 1x TAE
materials.uncertainty_note: Cost assumes commercial concentrate; homemade TAE would be cheaper.

materials.item_name: GelRed nucleic acid stain
materials.uncertainty_note: Prorated from a small vial; alternative safe stains have different prices.

materials.item_name: DNA ladder suitable for 1–2 kb bands
materials.uncertainty_note: Prorated from a 100-load ladder vial.

materials.item_name: 6x DNA loading dye
materials.uncertainty_note: Often supplied with ladder or PCR reagents; listed separately for completeness.

materials.item_name: SC-URA liquid medium components for engineered seed culture
materials.quantity: 25 mL plus losses, approximately 50 mL prepared
materials.uncertainty_note: Prorated from yeast nitrogen base, dropout mix and glucose stock.

materials.item_name: YNB-CSM liquid medium components for parental seed culture
materials.quantity: 25 mL plus losses, approximately 50 mL prepared
materials.uncertainty_note: Medium composition for the parental nonselective seed should be confirmed before preparation.

materials.item_name: Resazurin sodium salt anaerobic indicator
materials.quantity: 1 mg/L in approximately 600 mL, plus stock allowance
materials.uncertainty_note: Prorated from a small bottle; actual purchase price is much higher.

materials.item_name: 99.999% nitrogen gas
materials.quantity: approximately 12 L used, one cylinder rental/fill allowance
materials.uncertainty_note: Gas cost is dominated by cylinder rental/fill charges rather than the small volume consumed.

materials.item_name: Sterile 0.9% w/v sodium chloride solution
materials.uncertainty_note: Could be prepared in-house from NaCl at lower cost.

materials.item_name: Sulfuric acid for 5 mM HPLC mobile phase
materials.quantity: concentrated acid aliquot to prepare 1 L of 5 mM H2SO4
materials.uncertainty_note: Prorated from analytical-grade acid bottle; include institutional hazardous storage requirements.

materials.item_name: HPLC-grade water
materials.uncertainty_note: Cost assumes bottled HPLC-grade water; in-house polishing can be cheaper.

materials.item_name: Xylose analytical standard for HPLC calibration
materials.uncertainty_note: Analytical standard pricing varies by certified versus non-certified grade.

materials.item_name: Ethanol analytical standard for HPLC calibration
materials.uncertainty_note: Certified reference material costs more than standard HPLC-grade ethanol.

materials.item_name: Xylitol analytical standard for HPLC calibration/peak confirmation
materials.uncertainty_note: Xylitol is needed because the interpretation criterion includes xylitol yield, though the run step explicitly names xylose and ethanol curves.

materials.item_name: HPLC column storage/wash solvent allowance
materials.quantity: small allowance for column equilibration and post-run wash
materials.uncertainty_note: Exact solvent needs depend on local Aminex HPX-87H maintenance SOP.

materials.item_name: 0.22 µm PES bottle-top filter
materials.uncertainty_note: Price depends on filter volume and sterile packaging.

materials.item_name: 0.22 µm PTFE syringe filter
materials.uncertainty_note: PTFE is specified for ethanol compatibility; verify pore size and sterility.

materials.item_name: Sterile syringes for lipid filtration and transfers
materials.uncertainty_note: Basic sterile disposable syringe pricing.

materials.item_name: Autoclavable media bottle, 1 L to 2 L
materials.uncertainty_note: Reusable glassware cost allocated per experiment rather than full purchase price.

materials.item_name: Sterile 50 mL baffled culture tube
materials.uncertainty_note: Baffled disposable tube prices vary by brand and cap type.

materials.item_name: 100 mm SC-URA agar plates with 20 g/L glucose
materials.quantity: 5 plates including transformation, 3 restreaks and spare
materials.uncertainty_note: Estimate assumes plates are poured in-house from listed medium components; pre-poured plates cost more.

materials.item_name: Sterile spreaders
materials.uncertainty_note: Includes transformation plating and spare spreaders.

materials.item_name: Sterile inoculation loops
materials.uncertainty_note: Includes colony picking, restreaking and PCR colony sampling.

materials.item_name: 250 mL baffled Erlenmeyer flasks
materials.uncertainty_note: Reusable flask cost allocated per experiment; disposable sterile baffled flasks would cost more.

materials.item_name: Breathable flask closures or sterile foam plugs
materials.uncertainty_note: Cost depends on reusable caps versus disposable closures.

materials.item_name: 120 mL pressure-rated serum bottles
materials.uncertainty_note: Reusable bottles may reduce per-experiment cost; verify pressure rating for fermentation.

materials.item_name: Butyl rubber stoppers for serum bottles
materials.uncertainty_note: Autoclavable anaerobic-grade stoppers are more expensive than standard septa.

materials.item_name: Aluminum crimp caps for serum bottles
materials.uncertainty_note: Single-use cap cost is stable; select cap size matching stopper/bottle.

materials.item_name: Sterile 0.2 µm gas filters for nitrogen sparging
materials.uncertainty_note: If a single upstream sterile filter is validated for all bottles, cost could be lower.

line_items.item_name: New Brunswick Innova 44R incubator shaker use/reservation
line_items.quantity: 3 days reserved plus culture incubation use
line_items.uncertainty_note: Internal recharge rates vary widely; estimate assumes an academic shared-equipment daily use fee.

line_items.item_name: Eppendorf BioPhotometer D30 OD600 reader use
line_items.uncertainty_note: Many departments do not recharge for OD readers; verify local core-facility policy.

line_items.item_name: Coy Type B anaerobic chamber use
line_items.uncertainty_note: Pricing depends strongly on whether the chamber is departmental or core-managed.

line_items.item_name: Agilent 1260 Infinity II HPLC system use
line_items.uncertainty_note: HPLC recharge can be per hour or per sample; estimate assumes one full academic-use day.

line_items.item_name: Laboratory autoclave cycle for media sterilization
line_items.uncertainty_note: Autoclave costs are often not recharged directly; estimate covers steam, maintenance and consumables.

line_items.item_name: pH meter with calibration use
line_items.uncertainty_note: Assumes existing meter and electrodes; replacement electrode costs not included.

line_items.item_name: Thermo Scientific NanoDrop One spectrophotometer use
line_items.uncertainty_note: Institutional access may be free; estimate is a typical shared-instrument recharge.

line_items.item_name: Eppendorf 5424R refrigerated microcentrifuge use
line_items.uncertainty_note: Centrifuge access is commonly free in-lab; fee represents shared-equipment planning cost.

line_items.item_name: Eppendorf ThermoMixer C use
line_items.uncertainty_note: Estimate assumes existing heat block/thermomixer with nominal daily access cost.

line_items.item_name: Thermo Scientific Heratherm IMC18 microbiological incubator use
line_items.uncertainty_note: Incubator charges are often bundled with lab overhead; estimate uses daily access cost.

line_items.item_name: Thermal cycler for colony PCR
line_items.uncertainty_note: Actual recharge may be zero if owned by the lab.

line_items.item_name: Agarose gel electrophoresis tank and power supply use

line_items.item_name: Blue-light or UV gel documentation imager use

line_items.item_name: Analytical balance use

line_items.item_name: Class II biosafety cabinet use for GMO yeast and sterile transfers

line_items.item_name: Bottle crimper/decapper for 20 mm aluminum seals

line_items.item_name: D-glucose for yeast media and SC-URA agar plates

uncertainty_notes: Prices are 2024–2025 academic planning estimates in EUR and exclude VAT, shipping, hazardous-goods surcharges, institutional overhead and minimum order quantities.

Large differences are expected if strains/plasmids, media stocks, glassware and instruments are already available in-house; listed costs include allocated use fees to make shared-resource costs visible.
The positive-control strain is underspecified in the protocol, and ergosterol plus HPLC standards/column-use costs should be verified with current supplier quotes before ordering.

phases.phase_name: Day 0: Instrument scheduling, medium and stock preparation, plasmid QC
bottlenecks: Innova 44R shaker, anaerobic chamber, OD600 reader, and HPLC must be bookable across the required window
Autoclave cycle availability and cooling time for 1.2 L medium can delay same-day prep
Lipid stock may require warming/mixing if ergosterol precipitates
Plasmid DNA concentration or purity below threshold would require re-prep or concentration

phases.phase_name: Day 1-2: Revival of parental CEN.PK culture
dependencies: Day 0: Instrument scheduling, medium and stock preparation, plasmid QC
bottlenecks: Frozen glycerol stock viability may be poor, requiring re-streak or second revival
Shaker space and correct 30 °C/200 rpm conditions are required overnight
Contamination discovered after overnight growth would force restart from stock

phases.phase_name: Day 2-4: LiAc/PEG transformation and 48 h SC-URA selection
dependencies: Day 1-2: Revival of parental CEN.PK culture
bottlenecks: Low transformation efficiency may yield fewer than 20 colonies and require repeat transformation
SC-URA plate quality or dryness can affect recovery
Incubator space for sealed GMO plates at 30 °C must be available for 48 h

phases.phase_name: Day 4-5: Clone isolation by restreaking and 24 h colony growth
dependencies: Day 2-4: LiAc/PEG transformation and 48 h SC-URA selection
bottlenecks: Small or mixed colonies may require an extra day before picking
Insufficient isolated colonies after restreak may require repeat streaking
Maintaining plasmid selection on SC-URA plates is required to avoid false candidates

phases.phase_name: Day 5: Colony PCR and agarose gel confirmation of xylA-positive clones
dependencies: Day 4-5: Clone isolation by restreaking and 24 h colony growth
bottlenecks: PCR failure from poor colony lysis or inhibitors may require repeat PCR
Thermocycler and gel rig availability can limit same-day confirmation
Ambiguous 1.3-1.4 kb bands may require re-amplification or sequencing confirmation

phases.phase_name: Day 5-6: Engineered and parental aerobic seed cultures
dependencies: Day 5: Colony PCR and agarose gel confirmation of xylA-positive clones
bottlenecks: PCR-positive colony must be available early enough to start overnight seeds
Seed cultures may not reach OD600 3.0-5.0 by 16 h, delaying fermentation start
Shaker crowding can alter aeration and growth rate

phases.phase_name: Day 6: Anaerobic fermentation bottle assembly, deoxygenation, OD normalization, and inoculation
dependencies: Day 5-6: Engineered and parental aerobic seed cultures
Day 0: Instrument scheduling, medium and stock preparation, plasmid QC
bottlenecks: Nitrogen sparging 12 bottles is serial and sensitive to gas regulator/filter availability
Coy anaerobic chamber and crimper access are needed during the setup window
Resazurin remaining pink or bottle leaks require re-sparging/resealing
OD600 normalization errors directly affect fermentation comparability

dependencies: Day 6: Anaerobic fermentation bottle assembly, deoxygenation, OD normalization, and inoculation
bottlenecks: Maintaining 30 °C/150 rpm shaker conditions for sealed bottles for 48 h
Bottle overpressure or leaking septa can compromise anaerobiosis and safety
Biological variability in xylose uptake may necessitate repeat triplicate fermentation

phases.phase_name: Day 8: Fermentation sampling, clarification, and HPLC sample preparation
bottlenecks: Sampling must occur close to the 48 h timepoint to avoid yield bias
Needle clogging, septum coring, or bottle pressure can slow sampling
Centrifuge and cold rotor availability are required for rapid clarification
PVDF centrifugal filters may clog if samples contain residual biomass

phases.phase_name: Day 8-9: HPLC organic acid/sugar/alcohol run and chromatogram review
dependencies: Day 8: Fermentation sampling, clarification, and HPLC sample preparation
bottlenecks: Agilent 1260/HPC-87H column availability for a 10 h uninterrupted sequence
Column equilibration, pressure instability, or retention-time drift can force reruns
Calibration curve failure below R² 0.995 requires fresh standards and reinjection
Autosampler vial evaporation or precipitates can compromise late injections

phases.phase_name: Day 9: Data analysis, statistical testing, interpretation, and results write-up
dependencies: Day 8-9: HPLC organic acid/sugar/alcohol run and chromatogram review
bottlenecks: Peak integration errors for xylose, ethanol, or xylitol require manual review
Triplicate outliers or non-normal data may require additional statistical handling
Missing positive-control or parental data prevents complete ANOVA/Tukey comparison
PI review may request repeat fermentation before final conclusions

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: Determine whether CEN.PK Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing Piromyces sp. xylose isomerase produces at least 0.4 g ethanol per g xylose consumed within 48 h under anaerobic batch fermentation.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
